definition: Buy-side and sell-side liquidity operate as PAIRED targets — when one side is swept, the opposite side becomes the draw on liquidity. This is the fundamental mechanism of how price delivery works.
depends_on: liquidity (BSL and SSL are the paired targets)
feeds_into: draw_on_liquidity (swept side eliminates one target, pairing identifies the NEXT target)
risk_management (SL at swept side, TP at paired target)
related_to: market_maker_model (MMM buy model sweeps SSL, targets BSL — that IS order pairing)
CRT_candle_range_theory (candle high/low are paired — sweep one, target the other)
core_principle: Every liquidity level has a PAIR. PDH ↔ PDL. PWH ↔ PWL. Asian High ↔ Asian Low. Equal highs ↔ Equal lows. When price sweeps one side of the pair, the OTHER side becomes the new target.
application: After a confirmed liquidity_sweep of SSL (sell-side swept), the draw on liquidity is BSL (buy-side). Your TP = the paired BSL level. This is HOW you determine TP — not from arbitrary R:R multiples but from the paired liquidity target.
why_it_works: Institutions sweep one side to fill orders, then drive price to the other side where more orders wait. The pairing IS the institutional order book in action.

bridge_integration.detection: After sweep detection, paired liquidity target computed from session level map (PDH↔PDL, PWH↔PWL, Asian High↔Asian Low).
bridge_integration.prompt_display: draw_on_liquidity field shows the paired target as the primary TP level. Explicitly shown in trade parameters.
bridge_integration.score_impact: Clear paired target with R:R >= 2.0 = +5 to execution score. No clear paired target = TP ambiguity penalty.
